name: Primary Sourdough Batter B
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove sourdough starter from the refrigerator and stir it well.
2. Take out 1 cup of the stirred starter and place it in a warm bowl (4 qt or larger capacity). Return the remaining starter to the refrigerator.
3. Add warm water to the 1 cup of starter and stir until well mixed.
4. Slowly add flour, stirring continually to blend the flour into the mixture well.
5. Continue stirring for 4-5 minutes or until the mixture is smooth and lump-free.
6. Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and place it in a warm, draft-free area for 12 hours of proofing.
7. During the proofing period, if a crust forms on top of the batter or any liquid separates, stir it back into the batter.
8. At the end of the proofing period, stir the batter thoroughly.
9. Take out 1 cup of the batter and return it to the starter container in the refrigerator.
Yield: 1 1/2 cups Primary Batter B for baking, 1 cup batter for starter
From Adventures in San Francisco by Charles D. Wilford, Gold Rush Sourdough Co, SF, CA, 1971. ISBN 0-912936-00-2.
